name: RADIATION PNEUMONITIS
definition: damage to lungs after radiation therapy depends on:

demographics.Path: depletion of surfactant (1 week to 1 month later), plasma exudation, desquamation of
demographics.Histo: permanent damage of endothelial + type I alveolar cells

dominant_finding: mild hazy homogeneous increase in attenuation obscuring vessel outlines (2-4 months
discriminating_features: changes usually within portal entry fields
patchy / confluent consolidation, may persist up to 1 month (exudative reaction)
mild hazy homogeneous increase in attenuation obscuring vessel outlines (2-4 months

general: changes usually within portal entry fields
patchy / confluent consolidation, may persist up to 1 month (exudative reaction)
CT: mild hazy homogeneous increase in attenuation obscuring vessel outlines (2-4 months
coalescing patchy consolidations (1-12 months after therapy) not conforming to shape of
nonuniform discrete consolidation (most common; 3 months to 10 years after therapy)
dense fibrous strands from hilum to periphery
solid consolidation with parenchymal distortion ← radiation fibrosis + atelectasis
